question.label: Fragmented calendar
question.name: Calendar is still fragmented — what's breaking it up most?
question.description: Drills into the specific source of fragmentation so the friction is concrete and actionable.

answer: A lot of energy went into firefighting and context switching.

assessment.note: She names reactive firefighting and context switching as the source of fragmentation, which signals mild lack of clarity in how work is being shaped.

new_queue.label: Opening pace check
new_queue.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?

new_queue.label: Priority clarity
new_queue.name: What are you actually focused on this week, and why those things?

new_queue.label: Visibility gap
new_queue.name: Where does your work feel least visible right now?

new_queue.label: Influence bottleneck
new_queue.name: What tends to slow decisions down when you need buy-in?

new_queue.label: Manager support
new_queue.name: What do you want more of from me right now?

new_queue.label: Next two weeks
new_queue.name: What would make the next two weeks feel steadier for you?

history.answer_excerpt: The push worked, but I do not want that pace to become normal.
I am less overloaded now, but my calendar is still fragmented.
The push worked, but I do not want that pace to become normal.
I am less overloaded now, but my calendar is still fragmented.
A lot of energy went into firefighting and context switching.
